questions.question: Keras Sequential API is used for:
options: Stacking layers linearly -- one input, one output, no branching
questions.explanation: Sequential is the simplest Keras model. For branching/shared layers, use Functional API.

questions.question: model.compile() in Keras sets:
options: The optimizer, loss function, and metrics used during training
questions.explanation: Must compile before fit(). model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='binary_crossentropy')

questions.question: The Keras Functional API is used for:
options: Complex architectures with multiple inputs, outputs, or shared layers
questions.explanation: inputs = Input(shape=(30,)); x = Dense(64)(inputs); outputs = Dense(1)(x); model = Model(inputs, outputs)

questions.question: EarlyStopping callback stops training when:
options: A monitored metric (val_loss, val_accuracy) stops improving for patience epochs
questions.explanation: Prevents overfitting by stopping before the model starts memorising training data.

questions.question: model.save_weights() vs model.save():
options: save_weights: saves only weights (small file). save: saves architecture + weights + optimizer state (larger file)
questions.explanation: save_weights is lighter. save is complete and can be loaded without redefining architecture.

questions.question: Keras callbacks are used for:
options: Custom actions during training: model saving, learning rate scheduling, early stopping, custom logging
questions.explanation: ModelCheckpoint, ReduceLROnPlateau, TensorBoard are the most common callbacks.

questions.question: TensorBoard provides:
options: Interactive visualisation: loss/metric curves, weight histograms, computation graph, embeddings
questions.explanation: tensorboard --logdir logs starts the web UI. Integrate with tf.summary or Keras.

questions.question: The difference between PyTorch and Keras for research:
options: PyTorch: more control, preferred for research. Keras: higher level, easier but less flexible.
questions.explanation: Most ML research papers use PyTorch. Keras (TF) is popular in industry and education.

questions.question: tf.data.Dataset is used for:
questions.explanation: .prefetch(tf.data.AUTOTUNE) overlaps data loading with training for maximum GPU utilisation.

questions.question: model.summary() shows:
options: Architecture: layer types, output shapes, parameter counts, and total trainable/non-trainable params
questions.explanation: Essential for debugging model size and verifying architecture before training.
